## Direction — Path α multi-polecat execution arc

After mg-b10a STOP (trip-wire #2; Hibi polytope obstruction), mg-ff7f scoping (`6b62a77`) reframed:

> *"Hibi polytope is **not** required as a geometric polytope. The actual need is the **τ-inversion distributive lattice on `LinearExt α`** (Brightwell §3.5.1) — a substantively smaller piece of mathematics, polecat-class not mathlib-PR-class."*

**Verdict AMBER leaning GREEN.** Six-ticket sequential critical path; ~1800-3000 LoC across 6-9 polecat sessions. Side benefit verified at distributive-lattice level: same τ-inversion infrastructure underpins both case3 axiom AND Brightwell axiom. **End state if executed: trust surface = mathlib + native_decide.** Two axioms eliminated.

PM committed Path α per `feedback_announce_destination_and_eta` (Daniel 2026-05-06: *"find the best way to take forward progress and don't get blocked"*). First execution ticket filed today (mg-dc9d Hibi-1). Subsequent tickets file as each lands clean.

## Path α critical path (per mg-ff7f §4.1)

1. **Hibi-1** *(in flight, mg-dc9d)* — τ-inversion distributive lattice on LinearExt α (typeclass). 250-350 LoC. Single polecat 600k cap.
2. **Hibi-2** *(post-Hibi-1)* — FKG-on-LE primitive (typeclass). 150-250 LoC.

6. **case3-port-3+4+axiom-removal bundle** *(post-case3-port-2)* — Step 2 band-restricted FKG sub-coupling + Step 3 reduction + axiom discharge. 300-600 LoC.

**Plus parallel Brightwell tickets** dispatchable off Hibi-2.

**Calendar ETA:** ~8-14 weeks for full execution including the case3 application chain. **Daniel-help leverage point:** §8 of mg-ff7f flags a bounded-enumeration bypass on the case3 residual range that, if Daniel resolves by paper rigor, collapses Path α from "build FKG-on-LE for the case3 axiom" to "build FKG-on-LE only for Brightwell" — quarter the LoC, unambiguous GREEN.

## Trajectory

**Today (2026-05-06) was a high-information day.** mg-b10a's STOP surfaced the Hibi polytope obstruction; mg-ff7f's scoping reframed it as combinatorial (τ-inversion distributive lattice, polecat-class not mathlib-PR-class). The destination is the same — drop case3 axiom — but the path is clearer + has a verified Brightwell-discharge side benefit. mg-8487 case3-port-1 landed as the foundational orientation lemma formalising Daniel's transitivity-of-majority argument from 2026-05-05.

**Direction shift since this morning's regen:** the Path α picture is *substantially better* than mg-b10a's STOP report suggested. mg-b10a polecat said "mathlib-PR-class, ~2000-4000 LoC, multi-polecat sequential bottleneck"; mg-ff7f polecat says "polecat-class, ~1800-3000 LoC across 6-9 polecat sessions, 8-14 weeks calendar." The reframing from "geometric Hibi polytope" to "combinatorial τ-inversion distributive lattice" is the substantive insight.

**Cumulative project state:** headline hypothesis-free since yesterday; one paper-internal axiom (case3) + Brightwell remaining + 5 native_decide. After Path α completes, only mathlib + native_decide remain. **Forum send unhold-ready** at the end of Path α; Daniel's "extreme axioms" reservation addressed at root.

**Lessons codified as feedback memory** (newest first): *Pre-execution dependency verification* (Daniel 2026-05-06 audit; extends latex-first); *PM announces destination + ETA + drives forward* (2026-05-06; non-blocking); *PM is mini-CEO by default* (2026-05-05); *Complexity / token-budget blow-up means wrong path* (2026-05-05; trip-wires); *Distinguish substantive-chunk vs parallel-cleanup outcomes* (2026-05-04); *N-poset is NOT an ordinal sum* (2026-05-04, T7); *Dedup against closed-arc memory* (2026-05-04); *Latex-first for math-simp* (2026-05-02). Active discipline anchors all hold.
